E1.Info: It's been over 3,700 years since a flash of light petrified nearly all human life. A 16-year-old genius named Senku Ishigami is suddenly revived to find himself in a world where all traces of human civilization have been eroded by time.
E1.Subtitle: available
E1.Title: Stone World

E2.Info: After discovering how to liberate people from stone, Senku and Taiju decide who they will free up first. A series of events influence them to liberate someone who is very strong.
E2.Subtitle: available
E2.Title: King of the Stone World

E3.Info: Senku does not trust the motivation of Shishio, so he will try to hide his plans from a new and unforeseen enemy for his plan to revive civilization.
E3.Subtitle: available
E3.Title: Weapons of Science

E4.Info: Senku makes gunpowder to defeat Tsukasa. The gunpowder explodes and a smoke signal is made. Yuzuriha notices another smoke signal. Senku must decide to either cause more explosions as signals but risk revealing his position to Tsukasa.
E4.Subtitle: available
E4.Title: Fire the Smoke Signal

E5.Info: Taiju and Yuzuriha flee from Tsukasa while taking Senku with them. It is discovered how Senku survived his first days after being freed from petrification.
E5.Subtitle: available
E5.Title: Stone World the Beginning

E6.Info: Senku awakens after being nearly killed by Tsukasa and suggests splitting up with Yuzuriha and Taiju so they can spy on Tsukasa. A girl fights Tsukasa but is defeated and trapped under a tree. Senku finds the same girl and helps her.
E6.Subtitle: available
E6.Title: Two Nations of the Stone World

E7.Info: Senku helps Kohaku bring hot water back to her village for her ill sister. Kohaku tells Senku about her village and that it has forty people. Senku is told he cannot enter the village. Senku explains to Chrome about the history of mankind.
E7.Subtitle: available
E7.Title: Where Two Million Years Have Gone

E8.Info: Senku wants to make antibiotics and he explains how to Chrome and Kohaku. Senku decides to use magnets to collect iron. A child named Suika offers to help Senku. Senku decides to make ramen to appease the villagers and use their manpower.
E8.Subtitle: available
E8.Title: Stone Road

E9.Info: Senku feeds the villagers ramen so they can help him make iron. Senku meets Asagiri Gen a man who has been unpetrified and is working for Tsukasa. Asagiri decides to help Senku. Senku wants to make a generator with the help of lightning.
E9.Subtitle: available
E9.Title: Let There Be the Light of Science

E10.Info: After Gen is brutally beaten in a surprise attack, Kohaku devises a plan to bring down the attacker once and for all.
E10.Subtitle: available
E10.Title: A Flimsy Alliance

E11.Info: Suika never wants to show her face for some reason that only a few know. Fortunately, Senku knows the solution to her annoying problem.
E11.Subtitle: available
E11.Title: Clear World

E12.Info: To recover the most problematic ingredient in order to create sulfonamides, Ginro, Chrome, Kohaku and Senku will head to a zone of maximum danger. What kind of enemy will stand before them?
E12.Subtitle: available
E12.Title: Buddies Back to Back

E13.Info: All that's needed to make the medicine is alcohol. The best way to get some is to win the Grand Bout, which the Senku and his kingdom will also need to do in order to keep Magma away from Ruri and give her the drug.
E13.Subtitle: available
E13.Title: Masked Warrior

E14.Info: After Magma's series of tricks to reach the final match, Chrome reveals a trick of his own. With a little extra help from Gen, Chrome just may be able to pull this one off.
E14.Subtitle: available
E14.Title: Master of Flame

E15.Info: After Senku's unlikely victory, quick marriage, and even hastier divorce, the kingdom of science is back to work to finally finish Ruri's medicine.
E15.Subtitle: available
E15.Title: The Culmination of Two Million Years

E16.Info: Ruri reveals the origin of the village's name and Senku learns of his father's fate.
E16.Subtitle: available
E16.Title: A Tale for the Ages

E17.Info: Byakuya and the astronauts' story continues, and Senku's connection to Ishigami Village and the Hundred Tales is finally revealed.
E17.Subtitle: available
E17.Title: A Hundred Nights and a Thousand Skies

E18.Info: Tsukasa's army is coming. Kinro struggles to hold the bridge against the deadly spearman Hyoga and his shock troops, but Senku and the villagers are ready with deadly new weapons of science.
E18.Subtitle: available
E18.Title: Stone Wars

E19.Info: Hyoga is disarmed, but the kingdom of science is still in danger - Ishigami village is in flames. And Tsukasa will soon know that Senku is still alive - How will the villagers fight off the coming invasion?
E19.Subtitle: available
E19.Title: To Modernity

E20.Info: Work begins on creating the components for Senku's cell phone, but spinning gold wire is proving to be quite the task! Luckily, Chrome and Kaseki 'invent' the water wheel and drastically improve the process!
E20.Subtitle: available
E20.Title: The Age of Energy

E21.Info: With stored electricity, Senku takes the logical next step and creates light bulbs. After a little trial and error, Senku displays the new lights on a Christmas tree!
E21.Subtitle: available
E21.Title: Spartan Crafts Club

E22.Info: Senku, Chrome, and Magma venture into a cave to search for Tungsten; a suspicious accident leaves Senku and Magma trapped at the bottom of a pit.
E22.Subtitle: available
E22.Title: The Treasure

E23.Info: Senku and the rest of the village get to work on making the components for the cell phone. It's hard work but everyone is pumped!
E23.Subtitle: available
E23.Title: Wave of Science

E24.Info: After a successful test of their new cell phone, Senku learns of a mysterious tale about a bee named Speaker who can play the voices of the dead.
E24.Subtitle: available
E24.Title: Voices Over Infinite Distance
